narration: Everyone says invest a little every month — dollar cost averaging, right? Sounds safe. But Vanguard ran 100 years of data and found it's costing you thousands.
visual: Person silhouette dropping coins one by one into a calendar with monthly slots; coins bounce off a growing green arrow chart; text overlay: THE $23,000 MISTAKE

narration: Vanguard studied every 12-month window since 1926. Lump sum investing beat dollar cost averaging 68% of the time — across the US, UK, and Australian markets.
visual: Three globe icons for US, UK, Australia spring in; a large 68% stat animates to full size in green; bar comparing lump sum win rate vs DCA win rate

narration: Here's the math. You've got $50,000. Invest it all today, and on average you end up $3,400 ahead versus dripping it in over 12 months. That gap keeps compounding.
visual: Two side-by-side growing bar charts: LUMP SUM taller in green vs DCA shorter in gray; $3,400 difference highlighted with an arrow; dollar signs raining down on lump sum side

narration: Here's why. Markets go up about 75% of all trading days. Every month you wait to invest, you're probably sitting out a gain — and fear has a $23,000 price tag.
visual: Grid of calendar days: 75% marked with small green up-arrows, 25% with red down-arrows; running tally shows missed gains; large text: FEAR COSTS $23,000

narration: Now — if you don't have a lump sum, investing each paycheck is great. The real trap is hoarding cash on the sidelines, waiting for the 'perfect' entry point that never comes.
visual: Piggy bank overflowing with cash sitting on a shelf labeled WAITING FOR THE DIP; vs. a growth chart steadily climbing; red X over the waiting piggy bank

narration: If you've got cash parked in savings waiting to invest, that's the most expensive hesitation of your life. Put it in today — then follow for the next one.
visual: Green upward investment chart with a person silhouette pushing a pile of money onto it; CTA card: TIME IN THE MARKET BEATS TIMING THE MARKET; green follow banner
